---
title: Configurating temporal energy supply systems  - A case study from green field festival organisation
author:
  - name: Thomas Kirschstein
    email: thomas.kirschstein@ikts.fraunhofer.de
    affiliation: Fraunhofer IKTS - CEM
    correspondingauthor: true
    footnote: 1
  - name: Lisa Plümer
    email: lisa.pluemer@ikts.fraunhofer.de
    affiliation: Fraunhofer IKTS - CEM
address:
  - code: Fraunhofer IKTS - CEM
    organization: Fraunhofer IKTS - CEM
    addressline: Leipziger Strasse 70/71
    city: Halle
    postcode: 06108
    country: Germany
footnote:
  - code: 1
    text: "Second affiliation: RhineMain University of Applied Sciences, Wiesbaden"
abstract: |
  This is the abstract.

  It consists of two paragraphs.
keywords: 
  - keyword1
  - keyword2
journal: "An awesome journal"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
linenumbers: false
numbersections: true
bibliography: references.bib
biblio-style: elsarticle-harv # author year style for natbib - use 'elsarticle-num' or 'elsarticle-num-names' for numbered scheme
classoption: preprint, 3p, authoryear # remove authoryear is not using `elsarticle-harv`
# Use a CSL with `citation_package = "default"`
# csl: https://www.zotero.org/styles/elsevier-harvard
output: 
  rticles::elsevier_article:
    keep_tex: true
    citation_package: natbib
---

Please make sure that your manuscript follows the guidelines in the 
Guide for Authors of the relevant journal. It is not necessary to 
typeset your manuscript in exactly the same way as an article, 
unless you are submitting to a camera-ready copy (CRC) journal.

For detailed instructions regarding the elsevier article class, see   <https://www.elsevier.com/authors/policies-and-guidelines/latex-instructions>

# Mixed-integer linear model for festival energy system design

In this section, we formulate the mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model that configures the **festival** energy system for a given site and planning horizon.
The model selects and sizes on-site technologies and their operation in order to cover the time-varying electricity demand while respecting technical constraints and minimizing total cost and greenhouse-gas emissions.

Conceptually, the model decides which supply and storage technologies to install (e.g. diesel generators, battery storage, photovoltaic panels) and how to operate them in each time step during the festival.
It balances demand and supply in every period, enforces capacity limits, and represents storage dynamics as a linear state-of-charge evolution over time.

### Sets and indices

- $T$: set of time periods, indexed by $t$.
- $G$: set of dispatchable generators (e.g. diesel or HVO gensets), indexed by $g$.
- $P$: set of non-dispatchable generation technologies (e.g. PV), indexed by $p$.
- $S$: set of electrical storage technologies (e.g. batteries), indexed by $s$.

### Decision variables

Investment (design) variables:

- $y_g \in \{0,1\}$: installation decision for generator $g$.
- $K^{gen}_g \ge 0$: installed power capacity of generator $g$ [kW].
- $K^{pv}_p \ge 0$: installed power capacity of non-dispatchable technology $p$ [kW].
- $K^{ch}_s, K^{dis}_s \ge 0$: charge and discharge power capacity of storage $s$ [kW].
- $E^{max}_s \ge 0$: energy capacity of storage $s$ [kWh].

Operational variables:

- $P^{gen}_{g,t} \ge 0$: power output of generator $g$ in period $t$ [kW].
- $P^{pv}_{p,t} \ge 0$: power output of non-dispatchable technology $p$ in period $t$ [kW].
- $P^{ch}_{s,t} \ge 0$: charging power of storage $s$ in period $t$ [kW].
- $P^{dis}_{s,t} \ge 0$: discharging power of storage $s$ in period $t$ [kW].
- $E_{s,t} \ge 0$: state of charge of storage $s$ at end of period $t$ [kWh].
- $P^{grid}_{t} \ge 0$: imported power from the public grid in period $t$, if allowed [kW].

### Parameters

- $D_t$: electricity demand in period $t$ [kW].
- $\Delta t$: length of a time step [h].
- $c^{inv,gen}_g$: specific investment cost for generator $g$ [EUR/kW].
- $c^{inv,pv}_p$: specific investment cost for PV or other non-dispatchable technology $p$ [EUR/kW].
- $c^{inv,ch}_s, c^{inv,dis}_s$: investment cost for charge and discharge power of storage $s$ [EUR/kW].
- $c^{inv,E}_s$: investment cost for storage energy capacity $E^{max}_s$ [EUR/kWh].
- $c^{fuel}_g$: variable fuel cost of generator $g$ [EUR/kWh].
- $c^{grid}_t$: electricity purchase price from the grid in period $t$ [EUR/kWh].
- $e^{fuel}_g$: emission factor of generator $g$ [kg CO2e/kWh].
- $e^{grid}_t$: emission factor of grid electricity in period $t$ [kg CO2e/kWh].
- $\eta^{ch}_s$, $\eta^{dis}_s$: charging and discharging efficiency of storage $s$.
- $P^{pv,avail}_{p,t}$: specific PV availability profile (capacity factor) for technology $p$ in period $t$ [–], so that $P^{pv}_{p,t} \le P^{pv,avail}_{p,t} \cdot K^{pv}_p$.
- $E^{min}_s$: minimum state of charge of storage $s$ [kWh].
- $E^{budget}$: upper bound on total emissions [kg CO2e].

### Objective function

We minimize the sum of investment cost, operational cost, and optionally a penalty on greenhouse-gas emissions:

$$
egin{aligned}
\min  Z 
= & \sum_{g \in G} c^{inv,gen}_g K^{gen}_g
+ \sum_{p \in P} c^{inv,pv}_p K^{pv}_p\
& + \sum_{s \in S} \left(
 c^{inv,ch}_s K^{ch}_s
+ c^{inv,dis}_s K^{dis}_s
+ c^{inv,E}_s E^{max}_s

ight)\
& + \sum_{t \in T} \left[
\sum_{g \in G} c^{fuel}_g \, P^{gen}_{g,t} \, \Delta t
+ c^{grid}_t \, P^{grid}_{t} \, \Delta t

ight].
\end{aligned}
$$

If emissions are treated with an explicit price or weighting factor, an additional term can be added to the objective.

### Constraints

#### Power balance

For every period, total generation, net storage discharge, and grid imports must cover the demand:

$$
\sum_{g \in G} P^{gen}_{g,t}
+ \sum_{p \in P} P^{pv}_{p,t}
+ \sum_{s \in S} \left( P^{dis}_{s,t} - P^{ch}_{s,t} 
ight)
+ P^{grid}_{t}
= D_t
\quad \forall t \in T.
$$

#### Generator capacity

Dispatchable generators are limited by their installed power capacities:

$$
P^{gen}_{g,t} \le K^{gen}_g
\quad \forall g \in G, \, t \in T.
$$

If a binary build decision is used:

$$
K^{gen}_g \le \overline{P}^{gen}_g \, y_g
\quad \forall g \in G.
$$

#### Non-dispatchable generation (PV)

PV or other non-dispatchable technologies are constrained by availability profiles:

$$
0 \le P^{pv}_{p,t} \le P^{pv,avail}_{p,t} \cdot K^{pv}_p
\quad \forall p \in P, \, t \in T.
$$

#### Storage power and energy capacity

Power rating limits for storage:

$$
0 \le P^{ch}_{s,t} \le K^{ch}_s
\quad \forall s \in S, \, t \in T,
$$
$$
0 \le P^{dis}_{s,t} \le K^{dis}_s
\quad \forall s \in S, \, t \in T.
$$

State-of-charge dynamics:

$$
E_{s,t} = E_{s,t-1}
+ \eta^{ch}_s \, P^{ch}_{s,t} \, \Delta t
- rac{1}{\eta^{dis}_s} \, P^{dis}_{s,t} \, \Delta t
\quad \forall s \in S, \, t \in T.
$$

Energy capacity bounds and cyclic condition:

$$
E^{min}_s \le E_{s,t} \le E^{max}_s
\quad \forall s \in S, \, t \in T,
$$
$$
E_{s,T_{end}} = E_{s,T_{start}}
\quad \forall s \in S.
$$

#### Emission budget (optional)

If the festival operator specifies a maximum emission budget, the model can include:

$$
\sum_{t \in T} \left(
\sum_{g \in G} e^{fuel}_g \, P^{gen}_{g,t} \, \Delta t
+ e^{grid}_t \, P^{grid}_{t} \, \Delta t

ight) \le E^{budget}.
$$

### Notation summary

```{r milp-notation-table, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
knitr::kable(
  data.frame(
    Symbol = c(
      "$t$", "$g$", "$p$", "$s$",
      "$T$", "$G$", "$P$", "$S$",
      "$D_t$", "$\Delta t$",
      "$y_g$", "$K^{gen}_g$", "$K^{pv}_p$",
      "$K^{ch}_s$", "$K^{dis}_s$", "$E^{max}_s$",
      "$P^{gen}_{g,t}$", "$P^{pv}_{p,t}$",
      "$P^{ch}_{s,t}$", "$P^{dis}_{s,t}$", "$E_{s,t}$",
      "$P^{grid}_t$",
      "$c^{inv,gen}_g$", "$c^{inv,pv}_p$",
      "$c^{inv,ch}_s$", "$c^{inv,dis}_s$", "$c^{inv,E}_s$",
      "$c^{fuel}_g$", "$c^{grid}_t$",
      "$e^{fuel}_g$", "$e^{grid}_t$",
      "$\eta^{ch}_s$", "$\eta^{dis}_s$",
      "$P^{pv,avail}_{p,t}$",
      "$E^{min}_s$", "$E^{budget}$"
    ),
    Description = c(
      "Time period index",
      "Dispatchable generator index",
      "Non-dispatchable generation technology index",
      "Storage technology index",
      "Set of time periods",
      "Set of dispatchable generators",
      "Set of non-dispatchable generation technologies",
      "Set of storage technologies",
      "Electricity demand in period $t$ [kW]",
      "Length of time step [h]",
      "Binary installation decision for generator $g$",
      "Installed capacity of generator $g$ [kW]",
      "Installed capacity of technology $p$ [kW]",
      "Charge power capacity of storage $s$ [kW]",
      "Discharge power capacity of storage $s$ [kW]",
      "Energy capacity of storage $s$ [kWh]",
      "Power output of generator $g$ in $t$ [kW]",
      "Power output of technology $p$ in $t$ [kW]",
      "Charging power of storage $s$ in $t$ [kW]",
      "Discharging power of storage $s$ in $t$ [kW]",
      "State of charge of storage $s$ at end of $t$ [kWh]",
      "Imported grid power in $t$ [kW]",
      "Specific investment cost of generator $g$ [EUR/kW]",
      "Specific investment cost of technology $p$ [EUR/kW]",
      "Investment cost of storage charge capacity [EUR/kW]",
      "Investment cost of storage discharge capacity [EUR/kW]",
      "Investment cost of storage energy capacity [EUR/kWh]",
      "Fuel cost of generator $g$ [EUR/kWh]",
      "Electricity purchase price in $t$ [EUR/kWh]",
      "Emission factor of generator $g$ [kg CO2e/kWh]",
      "Emission factor of grid electricity in $t$ [kg CO2e/kWh]",
      "Charging efficiency of storage $s$",
      "Discharging efficiency of storage $s$",
      "Availability factor of technology $p$ in $t$",
      "Minimum allowed state of charge of storage $s$ [kWh]",
      "Total emission budget [kg CO2e]"
    ),
    Type = c(
      rep("Index", 4),
      rep("Set", 4),
      rep("Parameter", 6),
      rep("Decision variable", 11),
      rep("Parameter", 11)
    )
  ),
  caption = "Notation used in the MILP formulation for festival energy system design."
)
```

# References {-}
